coll: I: [olim Hiersemann 500/26 (8 ff.) = Louvain G-134 – lost] ; <II>: ff. 1–6 (first two folios of the quire are missing) ; III: ff. 7–14 ; IV: ff. 15–22 ; V: ff. 23–30 ; VI: ff. 31–38 ; VII: ff. 39–46 ; VIII: ff. 47–54 ; IX: ff. 55–62 ; X: ff. 63–70 ; XI: ff. 71–78 ; XII: ff. 79–86 ; XIII: ff. 87–94 ; XIV: ff. 95–101 ; XV: ff. 102–109 ; XVI: ff. 110–117 ; XVII: ff. 118–125 ; XVIII: ff. 126–133 ; XIX: ff. 134–137

related_mss.label: Originally belonged together with the following

excerpt.as_written: ܐܣܝܖ̈ܐ. ܗ̇ܐ ܩܝ̈ܡܝܢ ܒܗܝܟܠܐ ܘܡ̈ܠܦܝܢ ܠܥܡܐ
note: Corresponds to Acts 5:25, the reading for the Saturday of the second week after Easter

excerpt.as_written: .ܫܠܡ ܒܥܘܕܪܢ ܡܪܢ ܩܖ̈ܝܢܐ ܕܚܕܒܫ̈ܒܐ ܘܕܫܒ̈ܐ. ܡܢ ܚܕܒܫܒܐ ܕܦܢܛܝܩܘ(ܣܛܝ) ܥܕܡܐ ܠܚܕܒܫܒܐ ܪܒܐ

note: Byzantine rite, with occasional additions of the readings marked as ‘according to the rite of the Syrians’

contents.label: General indications for the use of the lectionary.

desc_provenance.rights: Unless otherwise indicated, all metadata is copyright the authors and is released under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0), https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/.

para.as_written: ܐܬܟܬܒ ܘܐܫܬܠܡ ܣܘܢܟܨܪܝܢ ܗ̇ܢܐ ܕܩܖ̈ܝܢܐ ܡܢ ܦܪܟܣܝܣ ܕܫܠܝ̈ܚܐ. ܘܡܢ ܦܘܠܘܣ ܫܠܝܚܐ. ܐܝܟ ܕܠܗܕܪܐ ܘܐܝܩܪܐ ܘܬܫܒ‍(‍ܘܚܬܐ) ܘܩܘܠܣܐ ܕܬܠܝܬ(ܝܘܬܐ) ܩܕܝܫܬܐ ܘܫܘܝܬ ܒܐܘܣܝ‍(‍ܐ) ܕܐܒܐ ܘܒܪܐ ܘܕܪܘܚܐ ܕܩܘܕܫܐ ܒܡܥܪܬܐ ܕܐܒܐ ܨܠܡܘܢ ܚܒܝܫܝܐ ܡܪܝܐ ܢܫܟܢ ܠܢ ܨܠܘ̈ܬܗ܀ ܫܩ̣ܠ ܕܝܢ ܣܟܐ ܫܢܬ ܐܠܦ ܘܬܠܬܡ̈‍(‍ܐܐ) ܘܚܡܫܐ ܘܚܡܫܝ̈ܢ܇ ܒܐܒ ܝܪܚܐ ܝܘܡ ܬܠܬܐ ܒܫܒܐ ܒܬܫ̈ܥ ܫ̈ܥܝܢ ܟܬܒܗ ܝܥܩܘܒ ܚܛܝܐ ܘܠܐ ܫ̇ܘܐ ܠܖ̈ܚܡܘܗܝ ܘܠܐ ܩܫܝܫܐ ܐܠܐ ܥܠ ܖ̈ܚܡܘܗܝ ܕܐܠܗܐ ܬܟܝܠ ܐܢܐ. ܠܘ ܥܠ ܥܒ̈ܕܝ ܕܝܕ̇ܥ ܐܢܐ ܣܓܐ ܐܪܓܙܝܬ ܠܡܪܢܐ ܥܠܝ ܐܠܐ ܐܚ̈ܝ ܡܛܠ ܚܘܒܐ ܕܚܒܢ / ܡܪܢ ܨܠܘ̈ ܥܠܝ ܕܐܬܚܢܢ ܐܢܐ ܕܘܝܐ܀ / ܒܨܠܘܬܗ̇ ܕܝܠܕܬ ܐܠܗܐ ܕܫܠܝ̈‍(‍ܚܐ) ܘܕܢܒ̈ܝܐ ܘܕܟܠܗܘܢ ܩܕܝ̈ܫܐ ܐܡܝܢ܀

assoc_place.value: Cave of Aba Salomon the Recluse

note: Completed on 3 August, AG 1355 [= CE 1044] by the priest Jacob, son of Joseph, in the cave of Aba Salomon the Recluse (precise location is not known)

program.label: NEH Syriac Parchment Descriptions Grant
program.description: Catalogued as part of UCLA Library's "Identification and Description of the Syriac and Arabic Manuscripts at St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, Phase 1: Syriac Parchment Manuscripts" project (2022-2025), funded by the National Endowment for the Humanities. Principle cataloguers were Grigory Kessel (Lead cataloguer), Natalia Smelova (Associate cataloguer), and Vevian Zaki (Cataloguer for Arabic content in Syriac manuscripts).

program.label: Sinai Library Digitization Project, Phase 1
program.description: Digitized under the Sinai Library Digitization Project, Phase 1 (2018-2023), a collaboration of St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, with funding from the Arcadia Fund, the Ahmanson Foundation, and the Steinmetz Family Foundation. The project provided high-resolution images and basic description of the Arabic and Syriac manuscripts held in the Monastery’s library.
